feed.title: awalvie
feed.subtitle: a blog about my coding journey

entry.title: I’m back
entry.content:
Monday

I’m back. That’s it.

What am I up to right now you ask ? I’m making a game engine. What’s that you ask, it’s the engine used to make a game.

Sarcasm aside, I joined this server that I’d highly recommend you join as well, it’s a Discord server that has some of the smartest, funniest and nicest people I’ve had the pleasure of talking to, and one of them suggested that we make a game engine together.

A game engine is something that you use to make the actual game with, our game engine is not based on Pixels but Voxels, a Voxel as I describe to other people right now is basically a 3D Pixel with metadata, and because of that you can do a lot of cool stuff with physics because you can define the action of every particular voxel to some event and it looks pretty cool. We planned on writing it in Vulkan but hey, we both don’t know shid about Graphics and it takes close to a 1000, yes you read it right 1000 lines of code to draw a TRIANGLE on the screen with. So we switched to OpenGl, we were going to make it Cross-Platform, use Vagrant, to keep the build Environments same and use CMake for the actual buildiing, but we ran into problems, (could’nt expose the graphics card in the Vagrant Box and CMake is complicated :) ), so right now the project is just set up for Visual Studio, but I plan on using PreMake to automate that process. The Goal right now is just to get through the OpenGl-Tutorial to get a basic understanding of how to render stuff on the screen and then get going with making the engine.

The reason I’m doing this right now is because if not for this I would still be on GitHub trying to find an organization to contribute to, make a list out of them, take days to select one, don’t like the Org and repeat the process over and over and not be productive. This will teach me a lot of stuff, a lot of disciplines of computer science and i’ll finally understand how people go from c=a+b in C++ to Doom.

Also I’ve had to pick up Competitive-Coding, I don’t like doing the questions, but it’s important to get a job, a necessary evil, so i’ll also be posting any questions I did from now on.

Also I’ve gotten into reading again, I just completed reading about the exploits of Arthur Dent in the the Book The Hitchhiker’s Guide to the Galaxy by Douglas Adams and plan to set up the books page so that I can talk about the book, so look forward to it.

Today’s Progress:

  • Vertex Buffers
  • Vertex Shaders
  • Fragment Shaders
  • Started The Blog Again (feels good man)

Competitive Problems Done:

  • Cards
  • Girls and Boys
  • Build A Fence
  • Complete The Series (Easy)
Im Back Baby Kuzco GIF from Imbackbaby GIFs

Tuesday

I’m sorry, did not make a lot of progress today, I was really enjoying reading Killing Floor by Lee Child, really fun book, spent all the time reading and finished it the next day (I’m writing this after completing it), and hence was’nt able to make a whole lot of progress.

Did some questions, wrote the entry for yesterday,

Competitive Problems Done:

  • Happy Numbers1
  • Beehive Numbers
  • Onotole needs your help
  • A Game with Numbers

Thursday

A pretty chill day today nothing out of the ordinary, did a couple of problems in college and read up some OpenGl, sleeping early and waking up early is a great way to set yourself up for being productive and in general feeling good about yourself. Would highly recommend :)

Today’s Progress:

  • Matrices in OpenGL
  • Time Complexity

Competitive Problems Done:

  • Boredom
  • Worms
  • Graphs
  • I did also just read through the DFS and BFS algorithms, just need to practically implement them now.

entry.title: Week 3
entry.content:
Day 7 Sunday - Day 1 Monday

LFS IS DONE !!! Woo hoo… It took me working (mostly waiting) all of Sunday and then troubleshooting GRUB on Monday but it works. Honestly my personal opinion would be just to read the book because most of the work is just copy-pasting and making minor changes to some files. Chapter 6 is just following step after step and 7 and 8 are also pretty straightforward.

New thing(s) learned

  • How linux works
  • How linux works
  • How linux works

(it’s a pretty big deal so writing it down 3 times)

Day 2 Tuesday - Day 3 Wednesday

Been sick today and yesterday, probably because I am not eating healthy and because I wanted to finish LFS, I drank way too much coffee and did not really sleep most of Sunday and Monday. Planning on watching Chernobyl, been on the watch-list for a while now.

entry.title: Week 2
entry.content:
Day 1, Monday

Thoughts and Progress:

Was travelling, First Time Flying, was very excited. I was the last guy in my family that had never been in a flight. Though the flight was pretty short I did not end up really doing anything as most of the day was used up in making preperations for the trip. Also it does not help that I had to spend 2.5 hours in a car to get to my location. Traffic ami’rit bois. I’m in Delhi to pursue an internship for a month so most of the posts are going to be about that.

Day 2, Tuesday

Thoughts and Progress:

  • First day at the Intern, mostly excited and scared. Feel that I’m highly unqualified to be here.

Waited a bit and was introduced to my mentor. You know that you are at the right place and talking to the right person when the first thing that the guy asks you to make is LFS, yep, take it in, the first thing that the guy asks me to do is build Linux From Scratch. At this point of time two things cross my mind.

  • Holy hell these guys are good if the first things I’m asked to do is make LFS.
  • I’m screwed.

Because of LFS, I have to put DVWA on hold as it’s going to take up most of my time.

Oh I never mentioned DVWA previously did I, let me explain. Sticking to one thing for a long time has never been my forte. My interests lie in C and security but I’ve not really been able to fully commit to either, I have done a played a couple of CTF’s and partially made some projects in C(nothing original mostly using tutorials on the internet) and was trying my hand everywhere but not really getting good anywhere. That’s when I asked a friend of mine to help and mentor me. We together decided that Web Sec would be a good jumping off point for me and he then suggested that I solve DVWA and multillidae so that was what I had been doing the end of the last week, solved a couple of vulnerabilities in DVWA but got of work on LFS right now.

  • I met my mentor quite late as there was some misunderstanding in the HR and I was assigned to a network team (where I had no clue what anybody was talking about) so I was mostly doing DVWA and started a bit late with LFS, all I did was see how to set up LFS on Virtual Box and the requirements for LFS (basically that it needs a seprate Disk and how to make seprate disks on VirtualBox)

New thing(s) learned

  • Wireshark basics
  • Using proxy with Burp Suite
  • Using hydra for brute force attacks

Day 3, Wednesday

Thoughts and Progress:

  • Too….Much….Information….

To anybody who attempts LFS, I humbly salute you and know your pain. The LFS book is fantastic and written in great detail. The only problem is there is a lot of jargon and complicated stuff involved (redditors: it’s a great learning experience for beginners). There is a lot you need to know and be aware of. I worked with it for 7 hours today and only ended up reaching Chapter 5.10. Now I know that there are some people that are gonna say What a noob, it took you that long to get there, I can do it in 5 minutes first, realax and second it’s my first time attempting this so have some mercy on me.

  • If you are planning to build LFS this is how I’m doing it.

    1. Install VirtualBox
    2. Install ubuntu on it
    3. After installing power down the VM
    4. Go to Settings -> Storage of the VM
    5. Make a new Sata HDD by clicking on the + sign

This is the basic environment setup, then you should be able to follow through with the book, this is more convenient as you don’t need to have an external disk to make the LFS, you have the ability to save the state of your system, because as you will come to know it’s not adviced to power down your machine and you have the ability to make snapshots that you can revert to if you mess up. You can also use Vmplayer instead of VirtualBox.

Do try to make notes as you go through the tutorial as there is a lot of stuff to understand and go slow, don’t rush. Try to look up and understand every word that’s foreign to you, and keep taking snapshots at regular intervals (highly recommended).

Day 4 Thursday - Day 6 Saturday

Thoughts and Progress

  • Not very happy… I did all the work until Chapter 6 which was mostly reading and compiling…. a lot of compiling and a lot of waiting. It’s safe to say that I am now feeling proficient making (see what I did there) anything from source. I go to sleep after shutting down my device and was feeling very happy that I’d done my task so quickly. Wake up on Saturday, get my Coffee, boot up the Virtual Machine and lo and behold it just wont start. Spent most of Saturday looking at forums to try and fix this problem but found no solutions. So all in all I feel sad, annoyed and angry.

entry.title: Week 1
entry.content:
First, let me apologize for the crude english that you are about to read now. A purpose of this blog is also that I improve my writing skills so bear with me for a while as I git gud. Any suggestions that you might have for me you can always share with me (that is if anybody reads this in the first place). Also there are gonna be a lot of memes and some of them are unfunny apparantly (maybe because of my awful sense of humor) so beware cringe memes.

So what are these weekly post ?

I am currently attempting the 100-days-of-code challenge to help me improve my coding skills, get more aquainted with contributing to open source software and in general spend more time learning and doing productive things than wasting it on YouTube or Dota2 (hit-me-up-anytime-if-you-wanna-play). Also this will serve as a way of motivating me everday, as I will never want to be empty handed when I try to write a post every day. I’ll be writing these in a weekly format so as to not have way too many entries on the blog and also because there won’t be that much content to write about every day.

Day 1

Thoughts and Progress:

  • Decided to take on the 100-days-of-code challenge, so I thought why not take this chance to set up a blog. I asked a good friend of mine how I’m supposed to go about making it and he pointed me in the direction of jekyll, looked at the various templates availabe for jekyll on GitHub and Jekyll Site and because I am very very picky person (thanks dad), I endind up spending way too much time trying to find the perfect minimal theme.

  • Though I spent close to 2 hours browsing, I found this super awesome theme Indigo that I just intantly fell in love with and was super happy when I found that there was a fantastic tutorial for setting up this theme. Now as I had zero experience and as the tutorial was a little outdated (it had nothing to with me being retarded and not reading the tutorial correctly) it took me the whole day to figure out the tutorial and change some of the settings as per my needs.

  • Also made a simple script in python to generate a template blog post so that I don’t have to manually write it everyday.

#!/bin/python

import datetime
x = datetime.date.today()
time = datetime.datetime.now()
time = str(time)
time = time[0:16]
file_title = input("File-Title: ")
title = input("Input Title: ")
filename = str(x) + "-" + file_title + '.md'
description = input("Input Description: ")
text_to_write = '''---
title: "{0}"
layout: post
date: {1}
tag:
- 
star: true
category: blog
author: awalvie 
description: "{2}"
---
'''.format(title, time, description) 

print(text_to_write)

with open(filename, 'w') as file:
    file.write(text_to_write)

Link to work:

My Blog !!

New thing(s) learned:

  • How to use and make GitHub Pages
  • How to make Blog Posts
  • Basics of Jekyll

Thoughts:

  • Have been very enthusiastic since yesterday, as the stuff I’m doing is actually working.
  • Have a lot more respect for Web Developers than I previously did.

You thought I forgot about the memes did you !! Oh how wrong you were.

Day 2

Thoughts and Progress:

  • So I’ll be honest this officially happened before setting up the site but I decided it would be better to write it this way. I spent a little bit of time trying to make some changes to my site, Broke it, and then restored it back to the original settings (hallelujah git).

    You see the problem was that the site has two options Large and Small the default value is Small and it’s placed in the first file that you edit for conveniece, now as I am dumb, I never noticed this option and spent two hours on it when all I had to do was change small to large.

  • You’ll often find that I spend a lot of time doing research, trying to find the perfect method for everything and never end up actually learning it, this is especially true here. I was looking for active C and Python projects to contribute to on GitHub and made this list. Picking one thing out of so many was a nightmare for me, and again after talking to my friend and heeding his advice “Pick anything” I rolled the dice (not really) and chose NeoVim as the organization to contribute to because that is the one open source software I use everyday.


C Orgs:

wrk
numpy
nuklear
jq
rsyslog
radare2
redis
Strace
syslog-ng
NeoVim 
oss-fuzz
performancecopilot
riot 
emscripten

Python Orgs:

Wikimedia (doubtful)
Read The Docs
Matrix 
Submitty
Owasp (Lots of Projects)
OpenWisp
OpenChemistry
Mozilla
NumFocus 
Sympy 
Internet Archive
MetaBrainz
AeroSpace Research
Google API                  #Search for this on github
AppleSeedHQ
Coala
certbot
GNU Radio
Youtube-dl
ranger
salt

  • Well all I did today was read up on the wiki and try to understand how to contribute to NeoVim. After reading about it for a little while and understanding very little I wondered if this is the right way to go because I felt that it was way above my head, but you know motivation and all and I kept going.

  • Also tried to learn CMake as that is how NeoVim is built, I found a make and CMake a decided it would be best that I understand how it worked so I have a better understanding of Projects.

New thing(s) learned:

  • Been trying to learn CMAKE, found a really helpful explanation on this answer - This also has been super helpful - The Wiki is fantastic as well.

Day 3

  • Was travelling from my village back to my home. A 9-10 hour car journey. Was very tired and did not do a lot, although I did look up some tracks on Pluralsight, mostly because a friend recommended that the courses were decent and that you can get 1 Month Free using Visual Studio Dev Essentials.

Day 4-5

  • Tbh I did not do much all these days, I lazed around and Played Dota and spent some time with family. I want to be truthful on these updates so I will give only a real account of what I did and did not do.
entry.summary: First, let me apologize for the crude english that you are about to read now. A purpose of this blog is also that I improve my writing skills so bear with me for a while as I git gud. Any suggestions that you might have for me you can always share with me (that is if anybody reads this in the first place). Also there are gonna be a lot of memes and some of them are unfunny apparantly (maybe because of my awful sense of humor) so beware cringe memes.
